title: GraphSchemata
description: Nested graphs where NodeDefinitions define reusable node types (context/category/type/ports) and Nodes are instances referencing those definitions. Subnets are Nodes that contain child nodes/edges and define their own interface. GraphInput/GraphOutput/GraphProp are special UI boundary adapters generated deterministically from a subnet's declared inputs/outputs/props. Edges are in-scope only and connect named ports (no indexes/lanes). Channels default to 'main' when omitted.

Port.description: A named, typed port in a node signature. Multi-input ports accept multiple incoming edges with values collected in edge array order.

multi.description: If true, this port accepts multiple incoming edges. Values are collected into an array in edge array order.

Ref.description: Houdini-like reference expression. Can be used anywhere a value is allowed (especially Prop.value). Rename/move can break refs and that's expected.

Prop.description: A configuration parameter value on a node instance. Values may be literals or Ref objects.

value.description: Literal JSON value or a Ref object.

PropDefinition.description: A property schema in a NodeDefinition. Defines the shape and defaults for configuration parameters.

EdgeEndpoint.description: In-scope endpoint. node is a relative name/path within the current graph scope. port is a named port (src uses outputs, dst uses inputs).

Edge.description: Connects a source node output port to a destination node input port. No lanes/indexes. Channel defaults to 'main' if omitted.

channel.description: Optional channel namespace (e.g. 'main', 'error', 'control'). Default is 'main' if omitted.

Group.description: UI grouping of sibling nodes in the same graph scope.

NodeKind.description: Optional hint for tooling/UI. Execution semantics are defined by type. GraphInput/GraphOutput are special boundary adapters for subnets.

NodeDefinition.description: Defines a reusable node type with its context, category, type identifier, and port/prop schema. Instances (Node) reference definitions by type.

type.description: Unique type identifier, typically in format 'context/category/name' (e.g. 'js/math/add').

inputs.description: Input port schema for this node type.

outputs.description: Output port schema for this node type.

props.description: Property schema for this node type.

icon.description: Icon identifier for this node type (e.g., emoji or icon name from react-icons).

Node.description: A node instance in a graph. References a NodeDefinition by type. Subnets (nodes with child nodes/edges) define their own interface via inputs/outputs/props.

name.description: Node instance name, unique within its parent scope. Identity is name-within-parent-scope; rename/move is identity change.

kind.description: Optional. If omitted, tooling may infer 'subnet' when nodes/edges exist; otherwise 'node'.

props.description: Configured property values for this instance.

inputs.description: For subnets only: defines the external input interface and drives generation of GraphInput boundary nodes.

outputs.description: For subnets only: defines the external output interface and drives generation of GraphOutput boundary nodes.

nodes.description: Child nodes (subgraph). Presence implies this Node is a subnet.

edges.description: Edges connecting child nodes. In-scope only.

groups.description: Optional UI grouping of child nodes.

Graph.description: Top-level graph container with optional inline definitions and node instances. Like subnets, graphs can declare inputs/outputs/props for their external interface.

definitions.description: Inline node type definitions. Definitions can also be provided externally to an evaluator.

inputs.description: External input interface for the graph. Drives generation of GraphInput boundary nodes (@in/<portName>).

outputs.description: External output interface for the graph. Drives generation of GraphOutput boundary nodes (@out/<portName>).

props.description: Configuration parameters for the graph. Drives generation of GraphProp boundary nodes (@prop/<paramName>).

x-normative-rules: Identity: A node's identity is its name within its parent scope (paths are allowed by convention). Rename/move is an identity change; references must be rewritten by tooling.
Scope: EdgeEndpoint.node values are relative and must refer to nodes within the same graph scope (i.e., members of the current Node.nodes). No cross-scope edges.
No lanes: Ports are named; there is no index/lane dimension. Branching uses explicit output port names (e.g., true/false/caseA/default/error).
Fan-in: Multiple upstream values must be combined with explicit nodes (Merge/Collect/Join/etc.). No implicit merging into a single-typed input.
Channels: Edge.channel is optional. If omitted, it is treated as 'main'. Only serialize when non-main.
Subnets: Any Node with child nodes/edges is a subnet. Its public interface is defined by its inputs/outputs/props fields.

Boundary adapters (UI): For any subnet, GraphInput, GraphOutput, and GraphProp nodes are generated deterministically from the subnet's declared inputs/outputs/props (Strategy 1). They are special node kinds and not treated as normal nodes.

Deterministic generation: Generated boundary node names and ordering must be stable (e.g., '@in/<portName>', '@out/<portName>', '@prop/<paramName>', sorted by name) to support canonicalization/merkle hashing.

Validation: Edges must connect src.port that exists in src node outputs to dst.port that exists in dst node inputs (or that exists in the node type registry signature for that node's (context, type) if you choose registry-derived ports).
Canonicalization: Consider sorting inputs/outputs/props arrays by name and excluding UI-only metadata fields from hashing to keep content-addressable IDs stable across layout changes.
